text: `**` treated as single `*` (matched only one subdomain level): DNS wildcard `**` in network policies now matches multiple subdomains instead of being treated as a single `*`

meta.symbol: `**` treated as single `*` (matched only one subdomain level)

text: `fromrequires` and `torequires` fields were permitted: Stop using `FromRequires` and `ToRequires` fields in CiliumNetworkPolicy and CiliumClusterwideNetworkPolicy as they are no longer permitted
default was enabled (or different): The `authentication.enabled` flag now defaults to disabled, so mesh auth will not be active unless explicitly enabled

network policies select endpoints from local cluster only by default: Network policies now select endpoints from local cluster only by default; use `policy-default-local-cluster: false` to retain prior behavior (though this option will be deprecated)

meta.symbol: network policies select endpoints from local cluster only by default

text: `clustermesh.apiserver.tls.authMode` now defaults to `migration` mode
MCS-API CRDs are now installed by default; set `clustermesh.mcsapi.installCRDs: false` to opt-out
Stop using `CiliumLoadBalancerIPPool` with `apiVersion: cilium.io/v2alpha1`; migrate to `apiVersion: cilium.io/v2`
bgpv1 control plane and `ciliumbgppeeringpolicy` crd: Stop using `CiliumBGPPeeringPolicy` CRD and BGPv1 control plane; migrate to v2 CRDs
`--bpf-lb-proto-diff` flag existed: Stop using `--bpf-lb-proto-diff` flag as it has been removed
`--enable-recorder` and `--hubble-recorder-*` flags existed: Stop using `--enable-recorder` and `--hubble-recorder-*` flags as the PCAP feature has been removed
`--enable-session-affinity` flag existed: Stop using `--enable-session-affinity` flag as it has been removed
`--enable-internal-traffic-policy` flag existed: Stop using `--enable-internal-traffic-policy` flag as it has been removed
`--enable-svc-source-range-check` flag existed: Stop using `--enable-svc-source-range-check` flag as it has been removed
`--enable-node-port`, `--enable-host-port`, `--enable-external-ips` flags existed: Stop using `--enable-node-port`, `--enable-host-port`, and `--enable-external-ips` flags as they have been removed
`--enable-custom-calls` flag existed: Stop using `--enable-custom-calls` flag as it has been removed
`--enable-ipv4-egress-gateway` flag existed: Stop using `--enable-ipv4-egress-gateway` flag as it has been removed
`--egress-multi-home-ip-rule-compat` flag existed: Stop using `--egress-multi-home-ip-rule-compat` flag as it has been removed
`--l2-pod-announcements-interface` flag existed: Stop using `--l2-pod-announcements-interface` flag as it has been removed
Stop using `--aws-pagination-enabled` and use `--aws-max-results-per-call` instead
Stop using `clustermesh.enableMCSAPISupport` and use `clustermesh.mcsapi.enabled` instead
Stop using `encryption.strictMode.*` and use `encryption.strictMode.egress.*` instead
integer (seconds), e.g. `--unmanaged-pod-watcher-interval=15`: `--unmanaged-pod-watcher-interval` now requires duration format (e.g. `15s`) instead of integer seconds
Stop using workqueue_adds_total and use cilium_operator_k8s_workqueue_adds_total instead.
queue_name label: All renamed workqueue metrics changed label queue_name to name, so any PromQL or dashboard referencing queue_name will break.
Stop using cilium_agent_clustermesh_global_services and use cilium_agent_clustermesh_remote_cluster_services instead.
using --reuse-values during minor version upgrades: Do NOT use --reuse-values flag during minor version upgrades; instead save values to file and pass via -f.

loadbalancer/clusterip traffic dropped if destination port doesn't match provisioned service (north-south direction): LoadBalancer/ClusterIP traffic is now dropped if the destination port doesn't match the provisioned service in north-south direction, which may break traffic that previously worked.

meta.symbol: loadbalancer/clusterip traffic dropped if destination port doesn't match provisioned service (north-south direction)

text: ciliumnetworkpolicy cannot match reserved:init labels; use ciliumclusterwidenetworkpolicy instead: Stop using CiliumNetworkPolicy to match reserved:init labels; convert those policies to CiliumClusterwideNetworkPolicy by changing the resource type.

meta.symbol: ciliumnetworkpolicy cannot match reserved:init labels; use ciliumclusterwidenetworkpolicy instead

text: Stop relying on automatic inference of cluster name and ID for external workloads; explicitly specify them as parameters using Cilium CLI >=v0.15.8.

enable-endpoint-routes now automatically sets enable-local-node-route to false: Stop expecting local node routes to be active when enable-endpoint-routes is enabled; they are now automatically disabled.

meta.symbol: enable-endpoint-routes now automatically sets enable-local-node-route to false

text: Stop using the policy.cilium.io/proxy-visibility Pod annotation for L7 visibility; migrate to L7 policies instead.

meta.symbol: l7 visibility can be configured via pod annotation policy.cilium.io/proxy-visibility

text: gateway api v1beta1 resources (gatewayclass, gateway, httproute) are supported: Stop using Gateway API v1beta1 resources for GatewayClass, Gateway, and HTTPRoute; ensure v1 CRDs are installed and migrate resources to v1.

meta.symbol: gateway api v1beta1 resources (gatewayclass, gateway, httproute) are supported

text: tunnel protocol is automatically set to geneve in native routing mode with dsr+geneve enabled: Stop relying on automatic tunnel protocol selection when using native routing mode with DSR+Geneve; explicitly configure --tunnel-protocol=geneve or helm value tunnelProtocol=geneve.

meta.symbol: tunnel protocol is automatically set to geneve in native routing mode with dsr+geneve enabled

text: cilium_prepend_iptables_chain (no trailing s): Stop using the environment variable CILIUM_PREPEND_IPTABLES_CHAIN; use CILIUM_PREPEND_IPTABLES_CHAINS (with trailing S) instead.

bpf masquerade fails initialization with error logging when requirements aren't met: Stop relying on BPF masquerade silently falling back to iptables; it now fails initialization with error logging if NodePort in BPF or socket load-balancing is disabled.

meta.symbol: bpf masquerade fails initialization with error logging when requirements aren't met

text: Stop using cluster-pool-v2beta IPAM mode; use multi-pool IPAM mode for dynamic Pod CIDR allocation.
install-egress-gateway-routes flag is available: Stop using the install-egress-gateway-routes flag; datapath improvements have eliminated the need for additional routes in ENI environments.

tunnel option (tunnel=disabled, tunnel=vxlan, tunnel=geneve): Stop using the tunnel option; use routing-mode=native instead of tunnel=disabled, or tunnel-protocol=vxlan|geneve instead of tunnel=vxlan|geneve.
single-cluster-route flag is available: Stop using the single-cluster-route flag; it has been removed as it was long defunct and undocumented.
enable-k8s-event-handover and cnp-status-update-interval options are available: Stop using enable-k8s-event-handover and cnp-status-update-interval options; they have been removed.

meta.symbol: enable-k8s-event-handover and cnp-status-update-interval options are available

text: Stop using clustermesh.apiserver.tls.ca.cert and clustermesh.apiserver.tls.ca.key; use tls.ca.cert and tls.ca.key instead.

authentication.mutual.spire.install.agent.image and authentication.mutual.spire.install.server.image as plain strings: Stop passing plain string image values for spire agent/server; use structured definition with separate repository and tag fields.

Prometheus metrics are now enabled by default for cilium-operator and clustermesh kvstore; explicitly disable with operator.prometheus.enabled=false and clustermesh.apiserver.metrics.etcd.enabled=false if unwanted.
Stop using the tunnel Helm value; use routingMode and tunnelProtocol instead.
Stop using enableK8sEventHandover and enableCnpStatusUpdates Helm values; they have been removed.

cilium_policy_l7_parse_errors_total, cilium_policy_l7_forwarded_total, cilium_policy_l7_denied_total, cilium_policy_l7_received_total: Stop using cilium_policy_l7_parse_errors_total, cilium_policy_l7_forwarded_total, cilium_policy_l7_denied_total, and cilium_policy_l7_received_total; use cilium_policy_l7_total instead.
default zero, honoring upstream dns ttls: The default --tofqdns-min-ttl changed from 3600 to 0, so DNS TTLs from upstream servers are now honored by default.

meta.symbol: default zero, honoring upstream dns ttls

text: Cilium now writes to 05-cilium.conflist instead of the previous 05-cilium.conf.
--update-ec2-adapter-limit-via-api default false: The default for --update-ec2-adapter-limit-via-api changed from false to true, requiring ec2:DescribeInstances IAM permission.

matching traffic allowed without rerouting when no gateway nodes available: Egress Gateway Policy now drops matching traffic when no gateway nodes are available (previously allowed without rerouting).

meta.symbol: matching traffic allowed without rerouting when no gateway nodes available

text: Gateway API requires upgrading related CRDs to v0.6.x (ReferenceGrant changed from v1alpha2 to v1beta1).
Stop using auth.type in CiliumNetworkPolicy Ingress/Egress rules; use authentication.mode instead with values disabled, required, or test-always-fail.

agents did not auto-cleanup stale meshed cluster information after kvstore reconnection: Agents now auto-cleanup stale meshed cluster information after kvstore reconnection; upgrade clustermesh-apiserver before Cilium agents to prevent connectivity disruptions.

meta.symbol: agents did not auto-cleanup stale meshed cluster information after kvstore reconnection

text: deny policies did not always take precedence over allow policies; cidr-based deny could be overridden by overlapping allow policies: Deny policies now always take precedence over allow policies; CIDR-based deny blocks overlapping allow policies that were previously allowed.

meta.symbol: deny policies did not always take precedence over allow policies; cidr-based deny could be overridden by overlapping allow policies

text: cilium_host ipv6 assigned from native host interface: cilium_host IPv6 is now assigned from the IPAM pool instead of the native host interface.

meta.symbol: cilium_host ipv6 assigned from native host interface

text: sockops-enable option existed: The sockops-enable option has been removed; stop using it.
force-local-policy-eval-at-source option existed: The force-local-policy-eval-at-source option has been removed; stop using it.
The disable-cnp-status-updates option is removed in v1.15 with no replacement.
The cnp-node-status-gc-interval option is removed in v1.15 with no replacement.
Replace tls-client-cert-file with tls-hubble-client-cert-file for Hubble Relay.
Replace tls-server-key-file with tls-relay-server-key-file for Hubble Relay.
kube-proxy-replacement=strict, partial, or disabled: Replace kube-proxy-replacement values strict/partial/disabled with true/false.
The cilium endpoint regenerate command has been removed in v1.15.
cilium_operator_ipam_{available,used,needed}_ips: Replace cilium_operator_ipam_ips metric with cilium_operator_ipam_available_ips, cilium_operator_ipam_used_ips, and cilium_operator_ipam_needed_ips.

securitycontext now applies to container; use podsecuritycontext for pod-level: Hubble Relay's securityContext now applies to the container level, not the pod level; use podSecurityContext for pod-level settings.

meta.symbol: securitycontext now applies to container; use podsecuritycontext for pod-level

text: Stop using containerRuntime.integration; use bpf.autoMount.enabled instead.
Stop using encryption.keyFile, encryption.mountPath, encryption.secretName, encryption.interface; use their encryption.ipsec.* counterparts instead.
hubble.peerService.enabled is removed.
hubble.tls.ca, hubble.tls.ca.cert, and hubble.tls.ca.key are removed.
hubble.ui.securityContext.enabled is removed.
ipam.operator.clusterPoolIPv4PodCIDR and ipam.operator.clusterPoolIPv6PodCIDR are removed.
Stop using proxy.prometheus.enabled and proxy.prometheus.port; use envoy.prometheus.* instead.
disableEndpointCRD is now a boolean instead of a string; remove quotes from the value.
Cilium CLI v0.15.0+ uses Helm installation mode; classic mode is not supported with Cilium v1.14 and the two modes are incompatible, requiring uninstall/reinstall to migrate.
Stop using RTE_FUNC_PTR_OR_* macros; they have been removed from EAL.
rte_eth_dev_bonded_slave macro: Stop using RTE_ETH_DEV_BONDED_SLAVE; it has been removed from ethdev.
flow classification library and examples: Stop using the flow_classify library and its examples; they have been removed.
kernel network interface (kni) library and driver: Stop using the KNI library and driver; they have been removed.

meta.symbol: kernel network interface (kni) library and driver

text: rte_crypto_cipher_algorithm_strings, rte_crypto_auth_algorithm_strings, rte_crypto_aead_algorithm_strings, rte_crypto_asym_xform_strings: Stop using rte_crypto_cipher_algorithm_strings, rte_crypto_auth_algorithm_strings, rte_crypto_aead_algorithm_strings, and rte_crypto_asym_xform_strings; they have been removed from cryptodev.
Stop using the explicit SM2 xform parameter in asymmetric xform; it has been removed from cryptodev.

meta.symbol: explicit sm2 xform parameter in asymmetric xform

text: reserved_opts field in struct rte_security_ipsec_sa_options: Stop using the reserved_opts field from struct rte_security_ipsec_sa_options; it has been removed from security.
rte_ml_io_input_size_get and rte_ml_io_output_size_get functions: Stop using rte_ml_io_input_size_get and rte_ml_io_output_size_get; they have been removed from mldev.
cmdline_poll function: Stop using cmdline_poll; it has been removed from cmdline.
Stop passing attributes to rte_thread_create_control(); it no longer accepts them.
Replace rte_thread_setname() with rte_thread_set_name().
Replace rte_ctrl_thread_create() with rte_thread_create_control().
rte_cpuflag_numflags macro: Stop using RTE_CPUFLAG_NUMFLAGS; it has been removed.
user must provide rss_key_len when rss_key != null: You must now set rss_key_len in rte_eth_rss_conf when providing rss_key; it no longer defaults to 40 bytes.

meta.symbol: user must provide rss_key_len when rss_key != null

text: keys in rte_crypto_ecdsa_op_param and rte_crypto_sm2_op_param: Stop accessing ECDSA/SM2 keys from rte_crypto_ecdsa_op_param or rte_crypto_sm2_op_param; they are now in rte_crypto_ec_xform per-session.
moved to internal headers, not visible to applications: Stop accessing rte_security_ops and rte_security_ctx directly; they are now internal and not visible to applications.

meta.symbol: moved to internal headers, not visible to applications

text: time parameters removed: Stop passing time parameters to rte_pcapng_copy and rte_pcapng_write_stats; they have been removed.
rejects key-only input, handles only key=value: rte_kvargs_process now rejects key-only input; use rte_kvargs_process_opt for key-only support.

meta.symbol: rejects key-only input, handles only key=value

text: Both rte_kvargs_process and rte_kvargs_process_opt now reject NULL kvlist parameter.
no explicit alignment: rte_ipv6_hdr, rte_ipv6_routing_ext, and rte_ipv6_fragment_ext are now two-byte aligned, which may affect struct layouts and casts.
rte_icmp_type_echo_reply, rte_icmp_type_echo_request: Replace RTE_IP_ICMP_ECHO_REPLY with RTE_ICMP_TYPE_ECHO_REPLY and RTE_IP_ICMP_ECHO_REQUEST with RTE_ICMP_TYPE_ECHO_REQUEST.
Stop using __rte_ethdev_trace_rx_burst; it has been removed.
enable_llq, normal_llq_hdr, large_llq_hdr devargs: Stop using enable_llq, normal_llq_hdr, and large_llq_hdr devargs for the Amazon ENA driver; use the unified llq_policy devarg instead.
Stop using RTE_CRYPTO_ASYM_XFORM_TYPE_LIST_END and RTE_CRYPTO_RSA_PADDING_TYPE_LIST_END as they have been removed.
single-event function pointers in rte_event_fp_fps: Stop using single-event function pointers from rte_event_fp_fps as they have been removed.
bcc's built-in clang compiler that compiles bpf code at runtime: Stop using BCC's runtime compilation approach; migrate to libbpf with BPF CO-RE and pre-compile BPF code.

meta.symbol: bcc's built-in clang compiler that compiles bpf code at runtime

text: Stop relying on BCC's auto-generated structs and naming conventions for tracepoints; use libbpf's explicit struct definitions instead.

meta.symbol: bcc's auto-generated structs and naming conventions for tracepoints

text: bcc's code rewriting to read kernel data and fetch kprobe arguments: Stop relying on BCC's automatic code rewriting to read kernel data and fetch kprobe arguments; use libbpf's CO-RE access macros instead.

meta.symbol: bcc's code rewriting to read kernel data and fetch kprobe arguments

text: bcc's semi-object-oriented c code style for working with bpf maps: Stop using BCC's semi-object-oriented C API for BPF maps; use libbpf's direct map access that matches kernel behavior.

meta.symbol: bcc's semi-object-oriented c code style for working with bpf maps

text: bcc requiring manual boilerplate in user-space to set up bpf programs: Stop writing manual boilerplate user-space code for setting up BPF programs; use libbpf's BPF skeletons instead.

meta.symbol: bcc requiring manual boilerplate in user-space to set up bpf programs

text: bcc requiring kernel headers package installed on every target host: Stop depending on kernel headers package being installed on every target host; use libbpf CO-RE which does not require kernel headers.

meta.symbol: bcc requiring kernel headers package installed on every target host

text: bcc detecting compilation errors only at runtime after restarting the application: Stop relying on runtime-only detection of BPF compilation errors; use libbpf's pre-compilation which catches errors at build time.

meta.symbol: bcc detecting compilation errors only at runtime after restarting the application

text: You must manually increase the locked memory limit (e.g. via ulimit) when using libbpf, because libbpf no longer does this automatically.

libbpf_set_print(print_libbpf_log) with function returning int and taking (enum libbpf_print_level lvl, const char *fmt, va_list args): Stop using a libbpf print callback that returns int; the callback must now return void.

meta.symbol: libbpf_set_print(print_libbpf_log) with function returning int and taking (enum libbpf_print_level lvl, const char *fmt, va_list args)

text: libbpf struct-based sec(".maps") declarations: Stop using BCC map macros (BPF_ARRAY, BPF_HASH, BPF_PERCPU_ARRAY) and use libbpf struct definitions with SEC(".maps") instead.
#include <linux/...> kernel headers: Stop including individual kernel headers and include vmlinux.h plus bpf_helpers.h, bpf_core_read.h, and bpf_tracing.h instead.
tsk->parent->pid (direct pointer chaining): Stop using direct C pointer-chaining field accesses like tsk->parent->pid and use BPF_CORE_READ macro instead.
libbpf requires explicit map size specification: You must specify map size explicitly in libbpf; there is no default like BCC's 10240.

meta.symbol: libbpf requires explicit map size specification

text: bcc pseudo-c++ syntax (some_map.operation(some, args)): Replace BCC's pseudo-C++ map method calls (e.g. .lookup(), .update()) with libbpf helper functions (e.g. bpf_map_lookup_elem(), bpf_map_update_elem()).
events.perf_submit(args, data, data_len): Replace .perf_submit() with bpf_perf_event_output() and add BPF_F_CURRENT_CPU flag.
bcc auto-detects program type without sec(): All BPF programs must be annotated with SEC("section_name") macro in libbpf; BCC does not require this.

meta.symbol: bcc auto-detects program type without sec()

text: libbpf uses trace_event_raw_<name> (or kernel-provided type): Tracepoint context type in libbpf is trace_event_raw_<name> instead of BCC's tracepoint__<category>__<name> pattern.
libbpf uses __data_loc_ prefix (double underscore): Replace data_loc_ with __data_loc_ (double underscore prefix) for variable-length string fields in tracepoint context.

libbpf requires explicit key_size/value_size for perf_event_array, stack_trace, devmap, cpumap: PERF_EVENT_ARRAY, STACK_TRACE, DEVMAP, CPUMAP maps don't support BTF types for key/value in libbpf; you must specify key_size and value_size directly.

meta.symbol: libbpf requires explicit key_size/value_size for perf_event_array, stack_trace, devmap, cpumap

text: syscall kprobes named like `__x64_sys_kill` (on x64): Starting from kernel 4.17, syscall kprobe names changed from `sys_<name>` to `__x64_sys_<name>` (on x64) and similar architecture-specific prefixes on other platforms.
global variables must be initialized (even to zero): Global variables must be initialized, otherwise libbpf will decline to load the BPF application.

meta.symbol: global variables must be initialized (even to zero)

text: read-only globals must use const volatile to prevent compiler optimizations: Read-only global variables must be declared with `const volatile` to prevent the compiler from erroneously assuming zero values and inlining them.

meta.symbol: read-only globals must use const volatile to prevent compiler optimizations

text: mutable globals must not be marked as static: Mutable (non-const) global variables must not be marked as `static` for best compiler interoperability.

meta.symbol: mutable globals must not be marked as static

text: declaring the same global variable in bpf code and user-space code creates independent, unconnected variables: Stop declaring shared global variables in both BPF and user-space code; use skel->rodata, skel->bss, or skel->data members of the BPF skeleton instead.

meta.symbol: declaring the same global variable in bpf code and user-space code creates independent, unconnected variables

text: loops in bpf code must be marked with #pragma unroll unless targeting 5.3+ kernel: Stop writing loops in BPF code without #pragma unroll; add #pragma unroll before every loop or target kernel 5.3+.

meta.symbol: loops in bpf code must be marked with #pragma unroll unless targeting 5.3+ kernel

text: static functions can drop __always_inline (kernel 4.16+); consider using __noinline instead: Stop marking all BPF static helper functions as __always_inline; drop __always_inline or use __noinline for better code generation on kernels 4.16+.

meta.symbol: static functions can drop __always_inline (kernel 4.16+); consider using __noinline instead

text: bpf_hash(name, ktype, vtype): Stop using BPF_HASH macro; define an explicit struct with __uint(type, BPF_MAP_TYPE_HASH) inside SEC(".maps") instead.
bpf_array(name, type, size): Stop using BPF_ARRAY macro; define an explicit BPF_MAP_TYPE_ARRAY struct inside SEC(".maps") instead.
bpf_perf_output(name): Stop using BPF_PERF_OUTPUT macro; define an explicit BPF_MAP_TYPE_PERF_EVENT_ARRAY struct inside SEC(".maps") instead.
Stop using map.lookup(&key); call bpf_map_lookup_elem(&map, &key) instead.
Stop using map.update(&key, &val); call bpf_map_update_elem(&map, &key, &val, 0) instead.
Stop using events.perf_submit(ctx, data, len); call bpf_perf_event_output(ctx, &events, BPF_F_CURRENT_CPU, data, len) instead.
implicit #include <linux/...> kernel headers: Stop relying on BCC's implicit kernel headers; include a single #include "vmlinux.h" instead.
no includes needed (bcc): Stop writing BCC programs without includes; libbpf requires explicit #include <bpf/bpf_helpers.h> and #include <bpf/bpf_core_read.h>.
Stop directly accessing kernel struct fields with ptr->field->subfield; use BPF_CORE_READ(ptr, field, subfield) instead.
Stop using int kprobe__func(struct pt_regs *ctx, args); use SEC("kprobe/func") int BPF_KPROBE(kprobe__func, args) instead.
implicit section naming (bcc): Stop relying on BCC's implicit section naming; add an explicit SEC("...") annotation on every BPF program.
compile-time #define config substitution (bcc): Stop using compile-time #define for config values; use global const volatile variables instead.
generated skeleton loaded via name__open(), name__load(), name__attach(): Stop using BCC's runtime compilation; use the generated skeleton with name__open(), name__load(), and name__attach() instead.

meta.symbol: generated skeleton loaded via name__open(), name__load(), name__attach()

text: Stop reading global variables the BCC way; access them via skel->rodata, skel->bss, or skel->data instead.
Stop using bpf_trace_printk(); use bpf_printk() instead, which accepts up to 3 arguments.
static functions without always_inline: Static helper functions in BPF must be marked static __always_inline, not just static.
Stop using 'BondState' in 'show evpn es' output; use 'State' instead.
show all advertised paths including non-best paths only if addpath is enabled: 'show bgp advertised-paths' no longer shows non-best paths unless addpath is enabled for that peer.

meta.symbol: show all advertised paths including non-best paths only if addpath is enabled

text: ospfv3 no longer maintains full adjacency when mtu mismatch is detected: OSPFv3 FULL adjacency is no longer maintained when an MTU mismatch exists between neighbors.

meta.symbol: ospfv3 no longer maintains full adjacency when mtu mismatch is detected

text: keywords could be used as interface names in static route configuration: staticd now rejects keywords (like 'blackhole', 'reject', etc.) when used as interface names in route configuration; you must use a valid interface name instead.

meta.symbol: keywords could be used as interface names in static route configuration

text: pim designated router (dr) did not change when the router's address changed: PIM DR election now re-evaluates and changes the DR when a router's address changes, which may alter forwarding paths.

meta.symbol: pim designated router (dr) did not change when the router's address changed

text: segment-routing/traffic-eng no longer appears in configuration unless explicitly configured: Stop relying on 'segment-routing/traffic-eng' always appearing in the configuration output; it is now only shown when explicitly configured.

meta.symbol: segment-routing/traffic-eng no longer appears in configuration unless explicitly configured

text: ip rip send/receive version command behavior fixed: The 'ip rip send/receive version' command behavior has been fixed, which may change which RIP versions are accepted or sent on interfaces that previously relied on the buggy behavior.

meta.symbol: ip rip send/receive version command behavior fixed

text: configured via cli/frontend: Stop configuring `allow-external-route-update` via the old CLI path; it has been moved to the management frontend side.
ospf no longer installs routes for directly attached networks: Stop relying on OSPF installing routes for directly attached networks; OSPF no longer installs them.

meta.symbol: ospf no longer installs routes for directly attached networks

text: pim sparse-dense mode could treat a group as dense even with an rp configured: In PIM sparse-dense mode, if an RP is configured for a group, the group is now treated as sparse (not dense), which may change multicast forwarding behaviour.

meta.symbol: pim sparse-dense mode could treat a group as dense even with an rp configured

text: bgpd did not allow overriding remote-as per-neighbor: bgpd now allows overriding the remote-as setting on a per-neighbor basis, changing how neighbor AS configuration works.

meta.symbol: bgpd did not allow overriding remote-as per-neighbor

text: replace-as did not replace the actual local-as when used with confederation: When using replace-as with confederation, the actual local-as is now replaced; previously it was not, so configurations relying on the old non-replacement behavior will see different AS paths.

meta.symbol: replace-as did not replace the actual local-as when used with confederation

text: 'no activate' was not honored for dynamic neighbors in a peer-group: Dynamic neighbors in a peer-group now respect 'no activate'; previously they would activate even when 'no activate' was configured, so existing configs relying on that bypass will stop working.

meta.symbol: 'no activate' was not honored for dynamic neighbors in a peer-group

text: bgp now does not reject the route if confederation as matches peer as: BGP no longer rejects routes where the confederation AS matches the peer AS; previously such routes were rejected.

meta.symbol: bgp now does not reject the route if confederation as matches peer as

text: ospf nssa routers could originate type-5 lsas directly: OSPF NSSA routers no longer originate Type-5 LSAs directly; they must use Type-7 LSAs instead.

meta.symbol: ospf nssa routers could originate type-5 lsas directly

text: only positive time strings allowed: ospf6d now rejects negative time strings; only positive time strings are accepted.
bmp updates no longer prepend local-as to as_path: BMP updates no longer prepend the local-AS to the AS_PATH, which changes the AS_PATH content in BMP monitoring messages.

meta.symbol: bmp updates no longer prepend local-as to as_path

text: show bfd peers brief json output different from show bfd peers: The JSON output of 'show bfd peers brief' no longer matches 'show bfd peers'; scripts parsing the brief JSON output will break.

meta.symbol: show bfd peers brief json output different from show bfd peers

text: llgr stale routes are not advertised to non-llgr peers: Stop relying on LLGR stale routes being sent to non-LLGR peers; they are now suppressed.

meta.symbol: llgr stale routes are not advertised to non-llgr peers

text: if multiple mpls labels were received, only the bottom label was used: Stop assuming BGP Labeled Unicast only uses the bottom label; up to 10 MPLS labels per prefix are now supported.

meta.symbol: if multiple mpls labels were received, only the bottom label was used

text: ospf ssl1 support: OSPF no longer supports SSL1; only SSL3 is supported.
confederation asns are no longer placed into as-set, left as alone: Stop relying on confederation ASNs being placed into AS-SET; they are now left alone and not included in AS-SET.

meta.symbol: confederation asns are no longer placed into as-set, left as alone

text: 'segment-routing ipv6' enabled by default when 'no segment-routing ipv6' is configured: The srv6-only command now correctly defaults when 'no segment-routing ipv6' is configured; previously it may have remained active despite the disable.

meta.symbol: 'segment-routing ipv6' enabled by default when 'no segment-routing ipv6' is configured

text: increased cli line length limit: The vtysh CLI length constraint has been increased, which may break scripts or automation that relied on the previous maximum line length.
Stop relying on the remoteTransposedSid field in JSON output of VPN paths; it has been reverted and removed.

meta.symbol: remotetransposedsid field in json output of vpn paths

text: Stop using the netns command in zebra; it has been deprecated and dropped.
bfd hold timer only starts if hold-time is configured (not zero): BFD hold timer now only starts if hold-time is explicitly configured (non-zero); previously it started unconditionally.

meta.symbol: bfd hold timer only starts if hold-time is configured (not zero)

text: rip does not send updates on disabled networks: RIP no longer sends routing updates on disabled networks; existing configurations relying on updates being sent on disabled networks will silently lose those updates.

meta.symbol: rip does not send updates on disabled networks

text: zebra now checks ra lifetime and interval for validity: Zebra now validates Router Advertisement lifetime and interval values; previously accepted values may now be rejected or cause different behaviour.

meta.symbol: zebra now checks ra lifetime and interval for validity

text: ospfd now correctly rejects virtual link creation on non-abr routers: OSPF virtual links can no longer be created on non-ABR routers; existing configurations that created virtual links on non-ABRs will now fail.

meta.symbol: ospfd now correctly rejects virtual link creation on non-abr routers

text: zebra cli commands for max-bw and related settings lack 'no' versions: Zebra CLI now supports 'no' forms for max-bw and related commands; existing scripts that used workarounds or different syntax to unset these values may break.

meta.symbol: zebra cli commands for max-bw and related settings lack 'no' versions

text: sending software-version capability even when explicitly disabled: The software-version capability is no longer sent if it has been explicitly disabled; previously it might still be sent despite being disabled.

meta.symbol: sending software-version capability even when explicitly disabled

text: rip/ripng default flush timer was changed in a prior version: The RIP/RIPng default flush timer change has been reverted, restoring the previous timer behavior.

meta.symbol: rip/ripng default flush timer was changed in a prior version

text: Stop relying on BGP IPv6 Link-Local Capability being enabled by default for datacenter profile; it is now disabled by default.

meta.symbol: enabled by default for datacenter profile

text: specified rd is no longer overridden: Stop relying on a user-specified RD being overridden; the specified RD is now preserved.

meta.symbol: specified rd is no longer overridden

text: disabled by default (as_set accepted): Stop relying on BGP accepting AS_SET by default; BGP now rejects AS_SET by default per RFC 9774.

malformed packets are handled the same way as for ebgp (withdrawing or discarding): Stop expecting iBGP session reset on malformed packets; iBGP now handles malformed packets by withdrawing or discarding them instead of resetting the session.

meta.symbol: malformed packets are handled the same way as for ebgp (withdrawing or discarding)

text: Stop assuming next hop weights are 8-bit; zebra now uses 16-bit weights for ECMP consistent hashing.
bmp does not send end-of-rib by default: BMP no longer sends End-of-RIB messages by default; you must explicitly enable this if needed.

meta.symbol: bmp does not send end-of-rib by default

text: bgp does not open a listening port for vrf instances: BGP no longer opens a listening port for VRF BGP instances; only the default instance opens a port.

meta.symbol: bgp does not open a listening port for vrf instances

text: soft-reconfiguration processing stalls bestpath processing until complete: Soft-reconfiguration no longer completely stalls bestpath processing; bestpath can proceed concurrently.

meta.symbol: soft-reconfiguration processing stalls bestpath processing until complete

text: irdp (icmp router discovery protocol) code in zebra: Stop using IRDP in zebra; the IRDP code has been removed entirely.

meta.symbol: irdp (icmp router discovery protocol) code in zebra

text: setting extcommunity link bandwidth value to zero was not allowed or had no effect: Setting extcommunity link bandwidth to zero is now permitted and will be used; previously it may have been rejected or ignored.

meta.symbol: setting extcommunity link bandwidth value to zero was not allowed or had no effect

text: `copy config from file` did not actually apply the config: Stop relying on `copy config from file` not applying the configuration; it now actually applies the copied config.

meta.symbol: `copy config from file` did not actually apply the config

text: evpn vni processing delay is now 10ms: The EVPN VNI processing delay has been reduced from 20ms to 10ms, changing timing behavior for EVPN route processing.

meta.symbol: evpn vni processing delay is now 10ms

text: clear log cmdline-targets (cli command): Stop using the CLI command `clear log cmdline-targets`; it has been moved to a YANG RPC.
bgp gr used keepalive message as implicit eor: Stop relying on keepalive messages being treated as implicit End-of-RIB for BGP Graceful Restart; this behavior has been removed.

meta.symbol: bgp gr used keepalive message as implicit eor

text: bgp session could start without a bgp identifier being set: BGP sessions no longer start when the BGP identifier is not configured.

meta.symbol: bgp session could start without a bgp identifier being set

text: non-default vrf cannot use table 254: Stop relying on non-default VRFs being able to use routing table 254; zebra now prevents non-default VRFs from using table 254.

`set evpn gateway-ip ipv4` and `set evpn gateway-ip ipv6` route-map commands worked as before: Stop using the previous broken behavior of `set evpn gateway-ip ipv4` and `set evpn gateway-ip ipv6` route-map commands; they have been fixed and may behave differently now.

meta.symbol: `set evpn gateway-ip ipv4` and `set evpn gateway-ip ipv6` route-map commands worked as before

text: ipv4 class e addresses (240.0.0.0/4) were not treated as connected routes: IPv4 class E addresses (240.0.0.0/4) are now treated as connected routes by default, which may cause routing table changes or unexpected behaviour if your network relied on these addresses being ignored.

meta.symbol: ipv4 class e addresses (240.0.0.0/4) were not treated as connected routes

text: system routes were selectable via 'ip protocol' route map match: Stop using 'ip protocol' route map match to match system routes; system routes are no longer available as a choice in 'ip protocol' route map filtering.

meta.symbol: system routes were selectable via 'ip protocol' route map match

text: Stop using the broken 'no ip rip split-horizon poisoned-reverse' command; the command was fixed so its behaviour now correctly removes poisoned-reverse split-horizon.
no srv6 (broken/incorrect behaviour): Stop using the previously broken 'no srv6' command; it was fixed so it now correctly disables SRv6.
static-sids (no way to remove): Stop using static-sids without a removal option; the 'no static-sids' command was added to remove static SID configuration.
bgp aggregate routes are no longer advertised to contributing ases: BGP aggregate routes are no longer advertised to the ASes that contribute to the aggregate.

meta.symbol: bgp aggregate routes are no longer advertised to contributing ases

text: `show bgp <afi> <safi>` no longer displays peers in groups: The `show bgp <afi> <safi>` command no longer displays peers grouped by peer-group.

meta.symbol: `show bgp <afi> <safi>` no longer displays peers in groups

text: rtadv remains enabled when bgp instance is unconfigured: Stop expecting rtadv to stay enabled after unconfiguring a BGP instance; it will now be disabled automatically.

meta.symbol: rtadv remains enabled when bgp instance is unconfigured

text: node-len could not be configured as 0: SRv6 node-len can now be configured as 0, which may cause existing configurations that previously rejected 0 to now accept it, potentially changing behaviour.

meta.symbol: node-len could not be configured as 0

text: ip[v6] forwarding was configured via legacy mechanism: Stop using the old legacy mechanism for configuring ip[v6] forwarding; use the new NB/mgmtd-based configuration path instead.

meta.symbol: ip[v6] forwarding was configured via legacy mechanism

text: router alert configuration is now required for pim: PIM now requires router alert configuration; existing setups without router alert configured will fail to operate.

meta.symbol: router alert configuration is now required for pim

text: v6 router advertisements are not sent anymore after interface up-down-up: V6 Router Advertisements are no longer sent after an interface up-down-up cycle, which may break IPv6 autoconfiguration on downstream hosts.

meta.symbol: v6 router advertisements are not sent anymore after interface up-down-up

text: srv6 encap-value displayed with '.' separator: SRv6 encap-value is now displayed with '.' instead of '_'; any scripts or monitoring parsing the old format will break.
gracefulrestartcapability json field in bgp output: Stop using the JSON field `gracefulRestartCapability` in BGP output as it has been dropped.

meta.symbol: gracefulrestartcapability json field in bgp output

text: per-daemon config files (e.g. bgpd.conf) were updated when 'write memory' was issued: Stop relying on 'write memory' to update per-daemon config files; use the integrated config at /etc/frr/frr.conf instead.

meta.symbol: per-daemon config files (e.g. bgpd.conf) were updated when 'write memory' was issued

text: sec("xdp1") or sec("xdp_prog1") style random suffixes: Stop using random suffixes in SEC() annotations; use only canonical annotation names like SEC("xdp") without suffixes.

meta.symbol: sec("xdp1") or sec("xdp_prog1") style random suffixes

text: Stop using SEC("maps") for map definitions; migrate to SEC(".maps") with BTF definitions.
c function names to uniquely identify bpf programs: Use C function names instead of section names to uniquely identify BPF programs.

meta.symbol: c function names to uniquely identify bpf programs

text: bpf program names used for pinning: BPF program names are now used for pinning instead of section names.

meta.symbol: bpf program names used for pinning

text: return negative -exxx directly and set positive errno to exxx: Integer-returning APIs now return negative -Exxx directly as result and set positive errno to Exxx.

meta.symbol: return negative -exxx directly and set positive errno to exxx

text: null pointers caused crashes in destructors like bpf_object__close() and btf__free(): Destructors like bpf_object__close() and btf__free() now safely accept NULL pointers without requiring guards.

meta.symbol: null pointers caused crashes in destructors like bpf_object__close() and btf__free()

text: xsk.h apis in libbpf: Stop using xsk.h APIs from libbpf; they have been moved to the separate libxdp project.
bpf_program__get_type() style getters with 'get_' prefix: Stop using get_ prefix in getter functions; use bpf_program__type() instead of bpf_program__get_type().
Stop using the six legacy map creation functions; use bpf_map_create() instead.
Stop using the multiple legacy program loading APIs; use bpf_prog_load() instead.
bpf_probe_read_{kernel,user}(): Stop using bpf_probe_read_kernel() and bpf_probe_read_user(); they are downgraded to bpf_probe_read() on older kernels.
libbpf_set_strict_mode() opt-in: Starting in libbpf v1.0, LIBBPF_STRICT_ALL behavior is the default; you must explicitly opt out if you rely on pre-1.0 loose behavior.
libbpf_strict_none (0x00) - disabling all libbpf 1.0 behaviors: LIBBPF_STRICT_NONE mode will not be supported in libbpf v1.0; you must update code to handle LIBBPF_STRICT_ALL mode.

pointer-returning apis (e.g. bpf_object__open_file, btf__parse, perf_buffer__new) return special non-null error pointers (err_ptr encoding error code): With LIBBPF_STRICT_CLEAN_PTRS, pointer-returning APIs return NULL instead of ERR_PTR error pointers; check for NULL and use -errno to get the error code.

meta.symbol: pointer-returning apis (e.g. bpf_object__open_file, btf__parse, perf_buffer__new) return special non-null error pointers (err_ptr encoding error code)

text: libbpf_get_error() is the recommended way to check pointer-returning apis for errors: libbpf_get_error() is going to be eventually deprecated and removed in libbpf 1.0; switch to NULL checks and errno.

meta.symbol: libbpf_get_error() is the recommended way to check pointer-returning apis for errors

text: return -1 on error with errno set: int-returning libbpf APIs now return -errno directly instead of -1; applications must check for < 0 instead of == -1.

meta.symbol: return -1 on error with errno set

text: libbpf always returns the actual negative error code directly and also sets errno: With LIBBPF_STRICT_DIRECT_ERRS enabled, libbpf always returns the actual negative error code directly instead of -1, so checks like 'err == -1' will break and must be changed to 'err < 0'.

meta.symbol: libbpf always returns the actual negative error code directly and also sets errno

text: High-level libbpf APIs (from libbpf.h and btf.h) now also set errno with a positive error code when returning a negative error code, making errno-based error handling possible but discouraged.
sec("xdp_cpumap"): The SEC annotation 'xdp_cpumap' is deprecated and must be replaced with 'xdp/cpumap'.
sec("xdp_devmap"): The SEC annotation 'xdp_devmap' is deprecated and must be replaced with 'xdp/devmap'.
sec("classifier"): The SEC annotation 'classifier' is deprecated and must be replaced with 'tc'.

return -1 with errno set, or return -exxx without setting errno: Low-level bpf_* APIs now return the negative errno value directly as the result, instead of returning -1 with errno set or returning -Exxx without errno.

meta.symbol: return -1 with errno set, or return -exxx without setting errno

text: In v1.0, non-conforming BPF program section names that previously logged a warning will now cause hard errors.

return err_ptr-encoded error on failure (non-null pointer with encoded error): Constructor APIs (bpf_object__open, btf__parse, bpf_program__attach, etc.) now return NULL on error instead of an ERR_PTR-encoded error pointer.

meta.symbol: return err_ptr-encoded error on failure (non-null pointer with encoded error)

text: libbpf_get_error() returned 0 for null (no error): libbpf_get_error() now returns -EINVAL when passed NULL, making NULL and ERR_PTR errors transparently handled.
xsk.c and xsk.h have been removed from libbpf and moved into the libxdp library.
af_xdp parts in libbpf (xsk.h, xsk.c): Stop using xsk.h and xsk.c from libbpf; they are removed and moved to libxdp.
Stop using non-'/' separators in SEC() names; use '/' consistently (e.g. 'perf_event/whatever' not 'perf_event_whatever').

allowing extra text beyond program type, e.g. 'xdp_my_prog' or 'cgroup_skb/ingress/garbage': Stop appending extra parameters to SEC() names; use only the program type (e.g. 'xdp') or type/subtype (e.g. 'cgroup_skb/ingress') with no extra text.

meta.symbol: allowing extra text beyond program type, e.g. 'xdp_my_prog' or 'cgroup_skb/ingress/garbage'

text: struct bpf_map_def sec("maps") { ... }: Stop using struct bpf_map_def in SEC("maps") for BPF map declarations; use only BTF-defined maps with SEC(".maps").
bpf_annotate_kv_pair(btf_map, int, struct ipv_counts): Stop using BPF_ANNOTATE_KV_PAIR macro; it is dropped in favor of BTF-defined maps with __type(key, ...) and __type(value, ...).
uses bpf program's c function name for filename part: BPF program pinning path now uses the C function name instead of the section name for the filename part.

meta.symbol: uses bpf program's c function name for filename part

text: multiple separate .rodata* maps (e.g., .rodata.str1.1): All .rodata* sections are merged into a single .rodata map, eliminating separate maps for each read-only section.
separate .bss map accessed via skel->bss: .bss is merged into .data, so all non-read-only variables are accessed through skel->data->my_var instead of skel->bss->my_var.
skel->bss pointer for .bss variables: skel->bss will be completely removed; bpftool will stop generating it after libbpf 1.0.
prefixed with truncated object name (e.g., 'my_ob.rodata'): Special internal map names no longer include the truncated object name prefix; they are just '.rodata', '.data', '.kconfig'.
btf__finalize_data() api function: btf__finalize_data() is deprecated and will be removed; it was only for internal libbpf use.
Stop using btf_ext__reloc_func_info() as it is deprecated and incompatible with multiple BPF programs per section.
Stop using btf_ext__reloc_line_info() as it is deprecated and being removed.
Stop using btf_ext__func_info_rec_size() as it is being removed; implement record size extraction yourself if needed.
Replace btf__load() with btf__load_into_kernel() to upload BTF into the kernel.

struct btf_dedup_opts (non-opts struct with dont_resolve_fwds and dedup_table_size fields): struct btf_dedup_opts is converted to an extensible OPTS struct; dont_resolve_fwds and dedup_table_size fields are removed.

meta.symbol: struct btf_dedup_opts (non-opts struct with dont_resolve_fwds and dedup_table_size fields)

text: btf__dedup() accepting old struct btf_dedup_opts: btf__dedup() now accepts a new OPTS-based struct; the btf_ext argument is supplied through opts instead of separately.
struct btf_dump_opts (non-opts) and btf_dump__new() accepting old struct: struct btf_dump_opts is converted to OPTS framework and btf_dump__new() now accepts the new OPTS-based struct.

meta.symbol: struct btf_dump_opts (non-opts) and btf_dump__new() accepting old struct

text: Stop using bpf_create_map_xattr(), bpf_create_map_node(), bpf_create_map_name(), bpf_create_map(), bpf_create_map_in_map_node(), and bpf_create_map_in_map(); use bpf_map_create() instead.
Stop using bpf_load_program_xattr(), bpf_load_program(), and bpf_verify_program(); use bpf_prog_load() instead.
Stop using bpf_prog_test_run_xattr() and bpf_prog_test_run(); use bpf_prog_test_run_opts() instead.
Stop using bpf_load_btf(); use bpf_btf_load() with OPTS instead.
Stop using bpf_object__open(), bpf_object__open_buffer(), and bpf_object__open_xattr(); use bpf_object__open_file() and bpf_object__open_mem() instead.
relaxed_core_relocs field: Stop using relaxed_core_relocs in struct bpf_object_open_opts; it is no longer honored.
Stop using bpf_object__load_xattr(); use bpf_object__load() instead, providing extra options via open opts or setter APIs before loading.
Stop using bpf_object__unload(); use bpf_object__close() to unload and free resources in one operation, as objects are not re-loadable after unload.
Stop using bpf_object__find_program_by_title(); iterate with bpf_object__for_each_program() and compare using bpf_program__section_name() instead.
Stop using bpf_object__next() and bpf_object__for_each_safe(); applications should track their own bpf_objects for iteration.
bpf_object__set_priv() / bpf_object__priv(): Stop using bpf_object__set_priv() and bpf_object__priv(); libbpf entities are not containers for user private data.
bpf_program__set_priv() / bpf_program__priv(): Stop using bpf_program__set_priv() and bpf_program__priv(); libbpf entities are not containers for user private data.
bpf_map__set_priv() / bpf_map__priv(): Stop using bpf_map__set_priv() and bpf_map__priv(); libbpf entities are not containers for user private data.
Stop using bpf_program__next(); use bpf_object__next_program() instead.
Stop using bpf_program__prev(); use bpf_object__prev_program() instead.
Stop using bpf_map__for_each(); use bpf_object__for_each_map() instead.
Stop using bpf_program__size(); use bpf_program__insn_cnt() to get the instruction count.
Stop using bpf_program__set_prep(); use bpf_program__insns() and bpf_program__insn_cnt() to get raw instructions, then load with bpf_prog_load().
Stop using bpf_map__resize(); use bpf_map__set_max_entries() instead.
Stop using bpf_map__def(); use individual getter APIs for map properties instead.
Stop using bpf_map__get_pin_path(); use bpf_map__pin_path() for consistent naming.
bpf_prog_load() / bpf_prog_load_xattr(): Stop using bpf_prog_load() and bpf_prog_load_xattr(); use bpf_object__open_mem() or bpf_object__open_file() followed by bpf_object__load() instead.

bpf_set_link_xdp_fd() / bpf_set_link_xdp_fd_opts() / bpf_get_link_xdp_id() / bpf_get_link_xdp_info(): Stop using bpf_set_link_xdp_fd(), bpf_set_link_xdp_fd_opts(), bpf_get_link_xdp_id(), bpf_get_link_xdp_info(); use libbpf_xdp_set_prog_fd() and libbpf_xdp_get_info() instead.
bpf_printk() using trace_printk: bpf_printk() now attempts to use static global functions, which can break on very old kernels; define BPF_NO_GLOBAL_DATA before including bpf_helpers.h if needed.
Stop using struct bpf_map_def for map definitions; use BTF-defined map definitions instead when LIBBPF_STRICT_MAP_DEFINITIONS is set.
all deprecated apis and features: Stop using all previously deprecated libbpf APIs and features; they have been removed entirely.
not supported: Stop relying on user-space ring buffer not being supported; it is now supported.

libbpf only adds bpf_f_mmapable flag for data maps with global (non-static) vars: Stop expecting BPF_F_MMAPABLE to be added for data maps with only static variables; it is now only added for maps with global (non-static) vars.

meta.symbol: libbpf only adds bpf_f_mmapable flag for data maps with global (non-static) vars

text: libbpf modified/transformed bpf verifier log level and flags before passing to kernel: Stop relying on libbpf transforming verifier log level/flags; they are now passed through to the kernel unchanged.

meta.symbol: libbpf modified/transformed bpf verifier log level and flags before passing to kernel

text: sec("tc"), sec("action"), sec("classifier"): Stop using SEC("tc"), SEC("action"), and SEC("classifier") as they are now legacy; use SEC("tc/egress"), SEC("tc/ingress"), SEC("tcx/egress"), or SEC("tcx/ingress") instead.

sec("struct_ops") and sec("struct_ops.link") auto-create struct_ops maps by default: SEC("struct_ops") and SEC("struct_ops.link") now default to NOT auto-creating the struct_ops map; use SEC("struct_ops") without '?' to get the old auto-create behaviour.

meta.symbol: sec("struct_ops") and sec("struct_ops.link") auto-create struct_ops maps by default

text: triple-underscore suffix (___.*) is ignored when resolving kfunc relocations: kfunc relocation resolution now ignores a triple-underscore suffix (___.*) on the function name, so a kfunc named 'foo___bar' will match a relocation for 'foo'.

meta.symbol: triple-underscore suffix (___.*) is ignored when resolving kfunc relocations

text: faccessat() used internally: The internal use of faccessat() has been fixed, which breaks Android versions of libbpf.
Stop using btf__get_map_kv_tids() as it is being deprecated and removed.
Stop using btf_ext__line_info_rec_size() as it is being removed; implement record size extraction yourself if needed.
Replace libbpf_find_kernel_btf() with libbpf_load_vmlinux_btf() for loading vmlinux BTF.
Stop using bpf_prog_attach_xattr(); use bpf_prog_attach_opts() instead.
Stop using attach_prog_fd in struct bpf_object_open_opts; use bpf_program__set_attach_target() on individual programs after opening the object instead.
Stop using bpf_map__next(); use bpf_object__next_map() instead.
Stop using bpf_map__prev(); use bpf_object__prev_map() instead.
Stop using bpf_program__title(); use bpf_program__section_name() instead.
Stop using bpf_object__find_map_by_offset(); it has been removed entirely.
Stop using bpf_program__pin_instance(); it is deprecated and will be removed.
Stop using bpf_program__unpin_instance(); it is deprecated and will be removed.
Stop using bpf_program__nth_fd(); it is deprecated and will be removed.
Stop using bpf_program__load(); it is deprecated because individual BPF programs cannot be loaded in isolation.
Stop using bpf_map__is_offload_neutral(); it is deprecated and likely broken.
Stop using bpf_perf_event_read_simple(); use perf_buffer__poll() and perf_buffer__consume() instead.
Stop using bpf_program__get_prog_info_linear() and related APIs; they will be renamed to libbpf_-prefixed names because they don't operate on bpf_program objects.
http_static_enable / http_static_enable_reply: Stop using http_static_enable and http_static_enable_reply; they are removed from the image.
af_xdp_create / af_xdp_create_v2: Stop using af_xdp_create and af_xdp_create_v2; they are removed from the image.
builtinurl_enable / builtinurl_enable_reply: Stop using builtinurl_enable and builtinurl_enable_reply; they are removed from the image.
session_enable_disable / session_enable_disable_reply: Stop using session_enable_disable and session_enable_disable_reply; they are removed from the image.
Stop using rdma_create_v3; use rdma_create_v4 instead.
Stop using ipsec_sad_entry_add; use ipsec_sad_entry_add_v2 instead.
Stop using gtpu_add_del_tunnel; use gtpu_add_del_tunnel_v2 instead.
Stop passing already-kernel-loaded xdp_program objects to xdp_program__attach() or xdp_program__attach_multi(); they will fail.

if kernel lacks incremental attach support (pre-5.10), the only way to run multiple programs is to attach them all at once with xdp_program__attach_multi(): On kernels older than 5.10, you cannot add a program to an interface that already has an XDP program; you must detach the existing program first or use xdp_program__attach_multi() to attach all programs simultaneously.

meta.symbol: if kernel lacks incremental attach support (pre-5.10), the only way to run multiple programs is to attach them all at once with xdp_program__attach_multi()

text: Stop using AF_XDP socket helper functions from libbpf; use libxdp instead as they have been moved there and will be removed from libbpf in the 1.0 release.
xsk_socket__create_shared() still requires privileges for AF_XDP socket creation, unlike xsk_socket__create() which can use a pre-created socket passed from a privileged process.

must use xsk_umem__extract_addr(), xsk_umem__extract_offset(), and xsk_umem__add_offset_to_addr() because the offset is carried in the upper 16 bits of addr: In unaligned mode, you cannot use the addr straight from the Rx descriptor; you must use xsk_umem__extract_addr(), xsk_umem__extract_offset(), and xsk_umem__add_offset_to_addr() to get the packet data pointer.

meta.symbol: must use xsk_umem__extract_addr(), xsk_umem__extract_offset(), and xsk_umem__add_offset_to_addr() because the offset is carried in the upper 16 bits of addr

text: poll() was the only way to check if wakeup is needed: When xsk_ring_prod__needs_wakeup() returns true, you must call recvmsg() for receiving or sendto() for sending instead of always using poll(), because poll() is slower.

meta.symbol: poll() was the only way to check if wakeup is needed

text: Stop using bpf_set_link_xdp_fd(); use bpf_xdp_attach() instead.
Stop using bpf_get_link_xdp_id(); use bpf_xdp_query_id() instead.
af_xdp apis from xsk.h in libbpf: Stop using AF_XDP-related APIs from libbpf's xsk.h; they have been consolidated into libxdp

meta.symbol: af_xdp apis from xsk.h in libbpf

text: Stop using bpf_set_link_xdp_fd_opts(); use bpf_xdp_attach() instead.
Stop using bpf_get_link_xdp_info(); use bpf_xdp_query() instead.

xsk.h af_xdp functions (xsk_umem__fd, xsk_socket__fd, xsk_setup_xdp_prog, xsk_socket__update_xskmap): Stop using the xsk.h AF_XDP API in libbpf; it has been removed and moved to libxdp (link with libxdp instead).

meta.symbol: xsk.h af_xdp functions (xsk_umem__fd, xsk_socket__fd, xsk_setup_xdp_prog, xsk_socket__update_xskmap)
